---
title: "Build a PTI"
---

> **This page is a placeholder.** A step-by-step guide to building and deploying a
> Project Targeting Index (PTI) application will be added here.

## Overview

Building a PTI application with `devPTIpack` involves three high-level steps:

1. **Prepare your data** — shapes (administrative boundaries) and a metadata
   Excel template describing your indicators.
2. **Validate your inputs** — run the built-in validators to catch problems
   before deployment.
3. **Launch the app** — use `launch_pti()` or `launch_pti_onepage()` to
   start an interactive Shiny dashboard.

## Step 1 — Scaffold a new project

Use `create_new_pti()` to copy the bundled template into a new directory:

```r
library(devPTIpack)
create_new_pti("path/to/my_pti_project")
```

*(Placeholder — full walkthrough of the project structure will be added here.)*

## Step 2 — Prepare geometries

*(Placeholder — guidance on formatting administrative boundary shapefiles to
match the `ukr_shp` convention will be added here.)*

## Step 3 — Prepare the metadata template

*(Placeholder — column-by-column description of the Excel metadata template
and how to fill it in will be added here.)*

## Step 4 — Validate inputs

```r
validate_geometries(my_shp)
validate_metadata(shp_path = "shapes.rds", mtdt_path = "metadata.xlsx")
```

*(Placeholder — interpreting validator output will be described here.)*

## Step 5 — Launch the app

```r
launch_pti(
  shp_dta  = my_shp,
  inp_dta  = my_metadata,
  app_name = "My Country PTI"
)
```

*(Placeholder — configuration options and deployment notes will be added here.)*
